quick_facts.label: Typical status and pay level
quick_facts.value: This is not a single, uniform all-India cadre: the employing department’s Recruitment Rules control its classification and method of appointment. For example, the Central Water Commission’s Farakka Barrage Project describes Assistant Engineer (Civil) as a General Central Service Group ‘B’, Gazetted, Non-Ministerial post in Pay Matrix Level 7 (basic-pay range ₹44,900–₹1,42,400 under the 7th CPC matrix).

quick_facts.label: Core role
quick_facts.value: In a Central Water Commission Assistant Engineer example, the officer heads a sub-division; supervises and executes work against approved designs, drawings and estimates; records/checks measurements; oversees maintenance; and submits progress and other reports to the divisional officer or higher authorities.

quick_facts.label: Main route from Junior Engineer
quick_facts.value: Promotion from Junior Engineer is a real central-government pathway, but eligibility and selection process differ by department. CPWD has conducted a Limited Departmental Competitive Online Examination for promotion from Junior Engineer (Civil/Electrical) to Assistant Engineer (Civil/Electrical), while the Department of Posts recorded promotions from Junior Engineer (Civil) to Assistant Engineer (Civil) in June 2026.

quick_facts.label: Qualification and service requirements
quick_facts.value: Department-specific rules can distinguish degree and diploma holders. A 2024 Delhi Milk Scheme notice for deputation to Assistant Engineer (Civil) required a Junior Engineer/equivalent with three years’ service for degree holders or seven years for diploma holders; other organizations may prescribe different conditions.

quick_facts.label: Career context
quick_facts.value: Assistant Engineer can be a feeder level for Executive Engineer or comparable posts. A 2025 Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology recruitment-rule notification lists nine years’ regular Level-7 Assistant Engineer service as one route for consideration to Executive Engineer (Civil), alongside other conditions.

sections.heading: What an Assistant Engineer does
paragraphs: An Assistant Engineer is commonly the supervisory link between Junior Engineers and the divisional engineering officer. In the Central Water Commission’s Farakka Barrage Project description, the Assistant Engineer heads a sub-division and is responsible for supervising and executing works in line with approved designs, drawings, estimates, norms and standards.

The same official description assigns measurement and quality-control-adjacent records work: taking measurements, checking work measured by Junior Engineers, examining standard measurement books, maintaining buildings/structures/areas/equipment, and furnishing progress and other reports to senior officers. The exact technical discipline and asset base—civil works, electrical systems, water infrastructure, estates or another function—depend on the department and sanctioned post.
bullets: Plan, supervise and document execution of assigned works.
Apply approved drawings, estimates, specifications and departmental standards.
Review Junior Engineer measurements and maintain prescribed measurement records.
Monitor maintenance work and prepare progress reports for the divisional hierarchy.

sections.heading: Entry routes from Junior Engineer and related routes
paragraphs: For this node, promotion from Junior Engineer is the principal route. The selection mechanism is department-specific: CPWD has used a Limited Departmental Competitive Online Examination for Junior Engineer-to-Assistant Engineer promotions, while other central organizations use promotion, deputation, or a composite method prescribed in their own Recruitment Rules.

Service and education thresholds are not portable from one department to another. For example, Delhi Milk Scheme’s 2024 Assistant Engineer (Civil) deputation notice recognized Junior Engineer/equivalent service of three years for degree holders and seven years for diploma holders. The Central Water Commission’s Farakka Barrage Project deputation description instead specified a civil-engineering degree and five years of regular Level-6 service for the stated route. Candidates should therefore read the recruitment rules, vacancy circular and promotion/LDCE notice for their own cadre rather than rely on a generic cutoff.

A distinct higher engineering-service route exists through UPSC’s Engineering Services Examination, but its advertised services commonly use titles such as Assistant Executive Engineer and Group A engineering-service appointments. It should not be treated as an automatic direct-entry route to every department’s Group B Assistant Engineer post.
bullets: Build regular service and a satisfactory record in the Junior Engineer feeder grade.
Check whether the department uses seniority-cum-fitness, a Departmental Promotion Committee, an LDCE, deputation, or a combination.
Verify the prescribed engineering discipline, degree/diploma treatment, qualifying service, vigilance clearance and any departmental examination requirements in the applicable notice.
For higher central engineering-service recruitment, track UPSC Engineering Services Examination notifications and the services/posts listed for that examination.

sections.heading: Skills to develop
paragraphs: The work description supports a practical skill profile centered on engineering execution and public-works controls: interpreting designs, drawings and estimates; site supervision; measurement and record verification; maintenance planning; and concise reporting to divisional authorities.

Because the Assistant Engineer checks work measured by Junior Engineers and ensures work conforms to approved standards, progression from Junior Engineer should include demonstrable accuracy in measurement books, estimates, drawings/specifications, work-progress monitoring and coordination with contractors, field staff and senior officers.
bullets: Engineering drawing, estimate and specification interpretation.
Site supervision, measurement, inspection and maintenance monitoring.
Measurement-book discipline, technical documentation and progress reporting.
Team oversight and clear coordination across Junior Engineers, contractors and divisional officers.

sections.heading: Where the role is performed
paragraphs: The post combines office-based engineering administration with field or asset-site work. The Central Water Commission example places the Assistant Engineer at sub-division level and explicitly covers execution, measurements, maintenance and reports—work that normally requires both site inspections and departmental-office documentation.

Location and working environment depend on the employer. Central-government Assistant Engineer roles may sit in project organizations, works/estate units, barrage or water-infrastructure offices, maintenance wings or other engineering establishments; postings and transfer liability should be confirmed from the relevant cadre rules and vacancy notice.
bullets: Sub-divisional or project engineering office.
Construction, maintenance and inspection sites.
Coordination with Junior Engineers and reporting through the divisional chain.

sections.heading: Progression after Assistant Engineer
paragraphs: Assistant Engineer experience can support movement into Executive Engineer or equivalent management roles where the department’s rules provide for it. In a 2025 MeitY notification, Executive Engineer (Civil) at Level 11 could be filled by promotion/deputation from a Level-7 Assistant Engineer (Civil) with nine years’ regular service, subject to the specified degree and construction/maintenance experience requirements.

This is an example, not a universal timetable. Promotion depends on the cadre’s recruitment rules, available vacancies, qualifying service, prescribed qualifications, selection process and other service conditions. Assistant Engineers should follow their department’s current seniority, DPC, promotion and recruitment-rule notices.
bullets: Develop progressively broader responsibility for design review, estimates, contracts, supervision and maintenance.
Maintain the engineering degree/experience profile required by the next promotional post where applicable.
Track cadre-specific eligibility rather than assuming a single central-government promotion schedule.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: As of the cited recruitment and vacancy documents, several central-government Assistant Engineer (Civil) posts are placed at 7th CPC Pay Matrix Level 7, whose basic-pay range is ₹44,900–₹1,42,400. This is a pay-matrix range, not a guaranteed take-home salary: monthly gross and net pay vary with admissible allowances, city/posting, deductions, deputation terms and future government pay revisions.

Classification also varies. The Central Water Commission example identifies a Group B Gazetted, Non-Ministerial post, and a MeitY recruitment-rule notification likewise lists Assistant Engineer (Civil) as Group B Gazetted, Non-Ministerial at Level 7. Candidates should use the specific advertisement or recruitment rules for the exact post to confirm pay level and status.
bullets: Treat ₹44,900–₹1,42,400 as cited Level-7 basic-pay context, not an in-hand-pay promise.
Confirm allowances, deductions, posting-related entitlements and deputation options in the department’s current notice.
Recheck pay details when applying because rules and allowances can change.
